properties.remark: NM DOT closed US 285 Relief Route on the west side of Roswell due to dust storm and near zero visibility.
Less than 1/4 mile visibility in blowing dust on video recorded by storm spotter.
Storm spotter recorded video showing visibility less than 1/4 mile in blowing dust.
Multiple vehicle accidents on NM state highway 2 between Dexter and Hagerman. Highway closed for multiple hours. Near zero visibility reported by responders and Dexter EMS.

*** 1 FATAL, 3 INJ *** Accident involving 7 vehicles on US 285 and Orchard Park south of Roswell. No visibility reported by responders. 1 vehicle caught on fire with 1 fatality. 3 others injured taken to the hospital. Responders could not see the burning vehicle in the dust. US 285 closed for multiple hours. Confirmed by county EM and state police.
Video shows near-zero visibility in blowing dust. Multiple vehicle crash likely occurred as a result. Highway 285 was closed in both directions as a result of the low visibility.
Dust storm producing a brown out at MM88 along U.S. Highway 285 west of Hagerman.
ASOS station KCQC Clines Corners 1 SSE.
Video shows tornado after it crossed State Route 4.

Corrects previous tornado report from 4 E Battleship Rock at an elevation of 8150 feet. Damage survey used video submitted to broadcast media to pinpoint the location of the tornado however the survey found few if any damage indicators for a damage rating. The only tree damage may have been from prior high winds and fire. A couple of ranches and residents along with a neighborhood to the SE of the location did not have any damages or downed trees in a pattern consistent with tornado damage. Other roads were restricted and the survey could not access areas where tornado damage was suspected. This report corrects the previous tornado report for location.

Damage survey used video submitted to broadcast media to pinpoint the location of the tornado however the survey found few if any damage indicators for a damage rating. A couple of ranches and residents along with a neighborhood to the SE of the location did not have any damages or downed trees in a pattern consistent with tornado damage. Other roads were restricted and the survey could not access areas where tornado damage was suspected. This report corrects the previous tornado report for location.

A haboob along an outflow boundary from storms to the west of Roswell caused blowing dust and extremely low visibility on U.S. 285 south of Roswell. The low visibility from the blowing dust resulted in two pileups on the U.S. Highway. one at Ojibwa Road and U.S. 285 and the other at Orchard Park and U.S. 285. Pictures shared from the Fire Chief of the Dexter Fire Department show several stalled vehicles and one extremely damaged car on the passenger side due to the pileup. The highway was closed by multiple agencies due to the pileups and the low visibility from the blowing dust.

*** 1 FATAL *** Two people were in the water at the Box Diversion Channel near Rachel Road S.E. and Four Hills Road. There was one loss of life and one rescue. A third individual was rescued near Wyoming Boulevard and Indian School Road. Time estimated.

Tijeras Arroyo overtopped about a 15 ft section of Via Sedillo on July 17th. A large volume of water pushed mud, rocks, and debris over the roadway. Several other sections of Via Sedillo were washed over with debris from overflowing culverts and driveways. Crews cleaned the roadway and improved drainage on July 19th.

*** 1 FATAL, 1 INJ *** Albuquerque Fire/Rescue were able to rescue a man and a dog in 7 feet of water flowing through the arroyo channel. The man refused treatment for injuries. Another man spotted in the arroyo channel was swept away and spotted lifeless in the channel near Menual Blvd and again near Osuna Rd. The body was recovered in the north valley outfall of the channel. Rainfall of 0.5 to 1.4 inches was reported upstream of the channel. Stream gage downstream reported a 7 foot rise.

Quay County Sun reporter relayed that up to 10 CMVs were blown over on I-40 due to high winds. A CMV driver estimated winds over 70 mph. I-40 was closed for several hours. Injuries were reported however the extent is unknown. Time estimated.

Quay County Sun reporter relayed that a roof was blown off the community center in Nara Visa. A social media post from Burns Ranch also showed a large section of metal fence lofted into the air and draped over a toppled tree. A second large tree was also down.

2 feet of water reported at the Burnt Well Guest Ranch. Roadways near the ranch flooded.
Media relayed report of water entering homes off of 2nd St. Widespread street flooding ongoing in the area.

*** 1 FATAL *** Emergency Management reports a fatality as a result of flash flooding in the Cahoon area near Spring River. Numerous water rescues have occurred and several cars have been swept under bridges. Several feet of water is entering houses nearby.
7 people rescued from vehicle swept away by water.
Multiple low water crossings along County Road A19A are flooded with several inches of water. Parts of San Pablo Creek and Tecolote creek have overflown its banks in the Mineral Hill area.
Delayed Report: Social media post showing destruction of a garage/shed and the roof being torn off another building. Estimated gusts surpass 90mph.
Penny to quarter size hail stripped trees of their leaves with 35 mph wind gusts.
Hail lasted for 15-20 minutes.
Numerous road closures in the Roswell area due to blowing dust reported via NM DOT. Roads impacted include US 380, US 285, US 70, NM 2, NM 246, NM 409 NM 254, NM 253 and the Roswell RR.
State Road 2 (Old Dexter Highway) from Tumbleweed Road to Orchard Park Road is flooded with water moving across the road.
State Road 2 (Old Dexter Highway) from Old Chisum Road to Anasazi Road is flooded.
Highway 285 South at Anasazi Road is flooded.
Neighborhood area near Ken Road (West of 285 South) is under 2 to 3 feet of water.
Video shows water flowing over Bottomless Lakes Road north of Bottomless Lakes State Park. Road was closed.
Water flowing over Highway 380 near Mile Marker 177.
Walnut creek was flooding onto State Road 2, forcing a road closure.
Water rushing over road near Rest stop 380 on Highway 380.
Measured 3.6 inches in gauge. Bar ditches are full, and Floyd Highway (NM 267) has several places where water is covering the road 6 to 12 inches deep, but the road is still passable.
Lots of hail covering the ground with stones ranging from half inch diameter to 1 inch diameter.

New Mexico State Police and Department of Transportation report closure of the Roswell Relief Route (U.S. 285 Bypass) on southwest side of Roswell between Sunset Ave and Brasher Rd (mile marker 108 to mile marker 110) due to blowing dust, brown-out conditions, and zero visibility.
